text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Willow Creek, there was a house so old it seemed to fade into the grass. Every night, the town would gather near the cemetery to hear the tale of its owner, Eleanor Frostblood. Eleanor had been a kind-hearted widow who lived alone in the crumbling mansion at the end of the lane.

One moonlit night, the town’s youngest storyteller, Jack, decided to visit Eleanor with hopes of hearing her legendary tales straight from the past. Eleanor sat by her fireplace, her fingers knitting a warm scarf absentmindedly. Jack leaned against the door post, his eyes twinkling with anticipation.

“You see,” Eleanor began, her voice soft but confident, “I once had a fox paw.”

Jack leaned forward, aghast. “A—fox paw? Isn’t that...?”

“A pet,” Eleanor chuckled. “But yes, a pet.”

“So, what happened?”

“The fox paw betrayed me.”
